Facets introduced CI Integration, an entity that provides a central repository for storing and managing Docker images for a service. CI Integration can be of type environment or release stream. Docker images can be stored via Facets CLI (facetctl) or promotion process. Facets CLI can be integrated with CodeBuild, Jenkins, CircleCI, or GitHub Actions.

Facets introduced promotion workflows in CI Integration. Promotion workflows can be of type environment or release stream and can be attached to similarly-typed CI Integration. They allow promoting builds from one environment to another (e.g., QA to Staging to Production). Promotion workflows include auditing information (who promoted which build and when).

The current implementation of the Artifact CI or CI integration page presents a challenge: users cannot attach a single promotion workflow to multiple Artifact CIs, which accounts for 90% of use cases. The page name may not accurately reflect its purpose. The process of attaching promotion workflows is tedious and requires editing each Artifact CI manually.
cannot attach single promotion workflow to multiple Artifact CIs
